domain: Enterprise cybersecurity email defense in a large organization’s inbound mail gateway
hypothesis: For enterprise cybersecurity email defense, a transformer-based text classifier trained on email body, subject, and attachment metadata will detect spear-phishing emails with higher PR-AUC than a baseline model using only sender/domain reputation and header-derived features.

domain: Emergency department operations using triage records, free-text chief complaints, and prior encounter history from a multi-hospital health system
hypothesis: For emergency department triage, a gradient-boosted model using only structured triage variables and prior utilization will be outperformed by a multimodal model that adds free-text triage notes and chief-complaint embeddings when predicting 24-hour hospital admission, measured on a future-time holdout.

domain: Retail banking anti-money-laundering monitoring for retail and small-business accounts
hypothesis: For retail banking AML transaction monitoring, a sequence model trained on customer-level transaction histories and counterparty network features will detect investigator-confirmed suspicious activity more accurately than a tabular gradient-boosted baseline using only aggregated account statistics, as measured by PR-AUC and alert yield at a fixed review capacity.

domain: Enterprise customer support operations for a B2B SaaS platform with ticketing, SLA, and agent workflow data
hypothesis: In enterprise customer support routing, a transformer model that uses the full ticket body plus prior customer-ticket history will predict first-response SLA breaches better than a baseline trained only on structured ticket metadata and agent-assigned priority, measured by PR-AUC on chronologically held-out tickets.

domain: Enterprise customer support knowledge base search for a SaaS product
hypothesis: For a large SaaS support organization, a transformer-based semantic search model fine-tuned on historical ticket–article click and resolution data will retrieve the correct help-center article for an incoming support query more accurately than a BM25 keyword baseline, especially for short, paraphrased, or typo-heavy queries.
Re] Reproducing Learning to Deceive With Attention-Based Explanations

domain: Healthcare analytics in a multi-hospital EHR system, using inpatient and outpatient records with longitudinal patient histories
hypothesis: For hospital readmission risk prediction, a sequence model trained on longitudinal EHR events and lab trajectories will outperform a baseline using only the most recent encounter’s tabular features in predicting 30-day unplanned readmission.
Re] Replication Study of 'Generative causal explanations of black-box classifiers

hypothesis: For enterprise cybersecurity email defense, a transformer-based text model that jointly encodes the email body, subject, sender history, and attachment metadata will detect phishing emails with higher PR-AUC than a gradient-boosted model trained on hand-engineered features alone, especially when evaluated on a temporally held-out quarter with novel attack campaigns.

hypothesis: For enterprise email security, a transformer model that incorporates sender-recipient communication graphs and historical message context will detect spear-phishing emails better than a text-only classifier, improving precision at a fixed low false-positive rate.

domain: Subscription streaming media customer analytics

domain: E-commerce marketplace search for a large catalog retailer with user query logs and product metadata
hypothesis: A transformer-based ranker using query text plus product title, attributes, and click history will outperform a BM25-only baseline for e-commerce product search relevance, measured by nDCG@10 on chronologically held-out traffic.

domain: Intensive care unit mortality prediction using electronic health records and clinical notes
hypothesis: A survival model that uses longitudinal clinician notes and medication changes from the first 72 hours of ICU stay will predict 30-day in-hospital mortality more accurately than a model using only structured EHR variables, when both are evaluated on future ICU admissions from the same hospital system.

domain: Cybersecurity incident detection for enterprise identity, endpoint, and email security operations
hypothesis: For enterprise cybersecurity incident triage, a temporal graph neural network that combines identity, endpoint, and email interaction events over the prior 30 days will detect true malicious incidents with higher PR-AUC than a gradient-boosted model trained on aggregated tabular alert features alone.

domain: Credit card fraud detection for a large consumer bank's card-not-present transaction stream
hypothesis: A transformer-based fraud model that ingests raw transaction sequences and device/session context will detect card-not-present fraud better than a gradient-boosted model trained on hand-engineered aggregate account features, as measured by out-of-time precision-recall performance at a fixed false-positive rate.

domain: Enterprise cybersecurity email defense in a large organization’s inbound mail gateway
hypothesis: For enterprise email security, a transformer-based classifier fine-tuned on full message text plus attachment and sender-history features will detect phishing emails with higher PR-AUC than a model trained only on header metadata and handcrafted URL/domain indicators.

domain: Healthcare / hospital readmission prediction using EHR data from an integrated health system
hypothesis: A survival model that incorporates longitudinal utilization features from the previous 12 months will predict 6-month patient readmission more accurately than a static baseline using only the last encounter’s demographics and diagnosis codes in a hospital system with frequent comorbidity progression.

domain: Enterprise SaaS customer retention analytics using product telemetry and billing data
hypothesis: A gradient-boosted survival model that uses event-level customer interactions and billing-history features will predict 90-day enterprise SaaS churn earlier and with higher concordance than a static logistic regression baseline trained only on last-month aggregate usage metrics.

domain: Education — early-warning prediction of student course failure in a university LMS or MOOC platform using clickstream, assignment, and gradebook data
hypothesis: A transformer-based model that uses the full sequence of prior student interactions and content metadata will predict end-of-term course failure more accurately than a static baseline logistic regression trained on aggregated features in a large online learning platform.

planted_issues.description: The use of stratified sampling across all terms instead of a chronological split allows future-term data to influence training, violating temporal integrity and creating an unrealistic evaluation of sequence modeling's ability to predict on new cohorts.

Fitting preprocessing (scalers, token vocabulary) on the complete dataset leaks future information into training, invalidating the comparison by granting both models artificial familiarity with held-out term distributions.

domain: Enterprise cybersecurity email defense in a large organization’s inbound mail gateway
hypothesis: For enterprise cybersecurity email defense, a transformer fine-tuned on full email text plus sender/recipient graph features will outperform a bag-of-words baseline on phishing and business-email-compromise detection, especially under a time-based holdout that simulates novel attack campaigns.

planted_issues.description: The test set is partially used during a deployment-readiness confirmation step to select the final model configuration. This invalidates the final holdout's independence for unbiased performance reporting, as the confirmation pass allows the model to fit to the test data distribution.

domain: Acute inpatient care in a multi-hospital EHR system with discharge planning workflows
hypothesis: For hospital readmission risk prediction, a model that combines longitudinal vital-sign and lab time series with discharge note embeddings will predict 30-day unplanned readmission better than a tabular model using only demographics, comorbidities, and summary lab statistics, as measured on a future-time holdout.

planted_issues.description: The baseline model uses a fixed hyperparameter configuration, while the hypothesis model undergoes hyperparameter tuning. This invalidates the comparison because any performance gain could stem from the hypothesis model's superior tuning rather than the added multimodal features. A fair comparison requires equal optimization effort for both models to isolate feature contributions.

domain: Healthcare / clinical ML for inpatient care management in an adult general hospital population
hypothesis: For hospital readmission risk prediction, a transformer model trained on the temporal sequence of prior admissions, medications, labs, and discharge diagnoses will outperform a logistic regression baseline on hand-engineered summary features in predicting 30-day unplanned readmission.

planted_issues.description: The analytic cohort was restricted to patients with at least 12 months of prior history, which excludes patients with sparse records and alters the target population from all adult discharges to only established patients. This creates a distributional shift that inflates apparent performance but does not reflect real-world deployment risks where newer patients may lack extensive history.

domain: Acute inpatient care in a large hospital network using electronic health records for ward and ICU patients
hypothesis: For hospital-acquired sepsis prediction from EHR data, a temporally aware sequence model trained on hourly vitals, labs, and medication administrations will predict onset within the next 6 hours more accurately than a traditional feature-engineered logistic regression baseline using aggregated last-observation-carried-forward features.

planted_issues.description: The primary metric evaluates patient-level AUC-ROC using maximum predicted risk across the entire risk window instead of horizon-specific forecasting. This obscures whether the sequence model provides earlier actionable warnings within the 6-hour lead time required by the hypothesis, conflating early detection with general sepsis risk stratification.

domain: Cybersecurity operations center (SOC) alert triage for enterprise network intrusion detection
hypothesis: For enterprise cybersecurity operations center alert triage, a learning-to-rank model that jointly uses SIEM alert text, entity graph features, and recent incident-response ticket history will rank true high-severity intrusion alerts above benign or low-priority alerts better than a text-only baseline, as measured by nDCG@10 on future alerts from held-out time periods.

planted_issues.description: The label definition uses analyst escalation or incident ticket linkage within 24 hours as the positive class, which measures early analyst workflow decisions rather than confirmed true intrusions. This misaligns with the hypothesis's focus on surfacing verified high-severity intrusion alerts, causing the evaluation to measure prioritization of process metrics (analyst responses) rather than ground-truth incident relevance.

hypothesis: Re] Replication Study of DECAF: Generating Fair Synthetic Data Using Causally-Aware Generative Networks

planted_issues.description: The experimental methodology was underspecified to the point that multiple interpretations of both the conceptual setup and the implementation were possible. The report states that reproducing the experiments required rewriting or adding large sections of code and that the provided code was incomplete, making the intended experimental design difficult to determine from the paper and artifacts alone.

The fairness evaluation used in the paper was not reproduced in numerically comparable form: although fairness metrics followed similar trends in the first experiment, their absolute and relative values did not match the reported results. This indicates that the paper's evaluation procedure or metric computation was not specified clearly enough for an independent implementation to recover the same fairness results.

The second experiment, involving synthetic bias injection followed by debiasing, could not be directly reproduced because the experimental setup required considerable changes relative to what was described or provided. The report explicitly notes that these changes make direct comparison difficult, suggesting that the original methodology for this experiment was insufficiently specified or operationalized for faithful replication.

Some reported findings were described as counterintuitive, including cases where algorithms not designed for debiasing appeared to debias data and sometimes outperform dedicated debiasing methods on the same dataset. This raises concern that the paper may have overinterpreted empirical outcomes or drawn broader methodological conclusions than were well supported by the experimental evidence.

domain: Enterprise cybersecurity intrusion detection using network flow logs and endpoint/network telemetry from a large corporate environment
hypothesis: A transformer model that uses both packet-level sequence features and session metadata will detect advanced persistent threat intrusions more accurately than a baseline gradient-boosted classifier built on aggregated flow statistics, as measured on next-day confirmation of malicious activity.

planted_issues.description: The preprocessing transformers are fit on the full 18-month telemetry corpus before splitting, which allows the model to exploit future data trends in validation/test periods. This invalidates the temporal separation, introducing bias and enabling detection of future attack patterns during training, undermining the evaluation of temporal generalization.

The primary metric (pAUC-ROC) poorly captures the operational requirement of managing alert fatigue: it rewards models that inflate false positives to gain marginally higher recall, while the domain requires high precision (low false positives) with acceptable recall. This divergence undermines the comparison's relevance to analyst capacity constraints.

domain: Consumer finance / credit card customer retention
hypothesis: For a consumer credit card issuer, a gradient-boosted model trained on raw transaction sequences and merchant-category embeddings will predict 30-day cardholder churn more accurately than a baseline using only aggregated spend and recency-frequency-monetary features, as measured on a future-month holdout.

planted_issues.description: The preprocessing steps (standardization, categorical vocabularies, and embedding initialization) use information from the full modeling corpus (including validation and test data) rather than being fit only on the training set. This allows future data to influence feature representation, creating temporal leakage and potentially inflating model performance estimates.

The baseline model uses a fixed regularization parameter without grid search, while the proposed model undergoes hyperparameter tuning. This unequal optimization effort leaves the comparison ambiguous: observed gains could stem from the transaction features or from differential model tuning, making the hypothesis test uninterpretable.

domain: Healthcare NLP — radiology report information extraction from retrospective clinical text across multiple hospital systems
hypothesis: In radiology report information extraction, a domain-adapted Transformer sequence-labeling model fine-tuned on raw report text will identify follow-up recommendation entities more accurately than a TF-IDF plus CRF baseline, improving span-level F1 on held-out reports from a different hospital network.

planted_issues.description: The primary metric has been changed from span-level F1 to report-level recall. The hypothesis asserts that the Transformer improves span-level accuracy, but report-level recall only measures whether a recommendation exists in a report, not whether the exact span is correctly identified. This mismatch invalidates the evaluation because a model could score well on report-level recall while generating incomplete or imprecisely bounded spans, directly undermining the hypothesis' focus on accurate entity extraction.

domain: Cybersecurity threat detection for enterprise account takeover investigation using identity provider logs, device telemetry, and email security events
hypothesis: For enterprise cybersecurity threat detection, a graph-based model that incorporates identity, device, and email interaction links will detect account-takeover incidents more accurately than a feature-only gradient-boosted baseline when evaluated on future incident windows.

ideal_debate_resolution.condition: Compare the graph model and baseline model's recall at the operational precision threshold (e.g., 0.6 or 0.8) and precision at the operational top-K budget (e.g., top 100 alerts per week) on the test set. If the model with higher average precision underperforms the alternative by more than 5 percentage points in either recall at precision floor or precision at top-K, average precision is insufficient as the primary metric.
ideal_debate_resolution.supports_critique_if: The graph or baseline model with higher average precision is worse by >5 percentage points in recall at the operational precision floor or precision at the operational top-K.
ideal_debate_resolution.supports_defense_if: The model with higher average precision is not worse by >5 percentage points in either metric.

required_empirical_test.condition: Compare the graph model and baseline model's recall at the operational precision threshold (e.g., 0.6 or 0.8) and precision at the operational top-K budget (e.g., top 100 alerts per week) on the test set. If the model with higher average precision underperforms the alternative by more than 5 percentage points in either recall at precision floor or precision at top-K, average precision is insufficient as the primary metric.
required_empirical_test.supports_critique_if: The graph or baseline model with higher average precision is worse by >5 percentage points in recall at the operational precision floor or precision at the operational top-K.
required_empirical_test.supports_defense_if: The model with higher average precision is not worse by >5 percentage points in either metric.

hypothesis: Re] Improving Multi-hop Question Answering over Knowledge Graphs using Knowledge Base Embeddings

planted_issues.description: The necessity of the Relation Matching (RM) module is contested by an ablation in which experiments were intentionally run without relation matching to verify its importance. This indicates that the architectural contribution of RM, as presented in the original method, may be overstated or insufficiently justified as essential to performance.

The claim of broadly reproducible performance is qualified by two benchmark anomalies: MetaQA-KG-Full (3-hop) and WebQSP-KG-Full did not match the reported behavior as closely as most other settings. This suggests the strength or uniformity of the reported gains across datasets and task settings may be overstated.

ideal_debate_resolution.condition: Most claimed results were reproduced within a small margin, but the report notes anomalies in two benchmark settings and questions the necessity of the relation matching module.

hypothesis: Re] Learning to count everything

planted_issues.description: The procedure for generating ground-truth density maps was not adequately specified and was described incorrectly, while the corresponding code was not released. This made the density-map construction process unverifiable from the paper and required independent reconstruction of missing details, creating ambiguity about a core preprocessing component of the method.

ideal_debate_resolution.condition: The main empirical claims were largely supported, but the report explicitly contests the paper's description of ground-truth density-map generation and notes missing implementation details for parts of the methodology.

domain: Cybersecurity operations center (SOC) alert triage using SIEM/EDR alert streams and incident response tickets
hypothesis: For enterprise cybersecurity SOC alert triage, a learning-to-rank model that uses incident-response ticket text plus SIEM/EDR alert metadata and historical case outcomes will prioritize true high-severity incidents more effectively than a binary classifier that predicts alert maliciousness and then sorts by predicted probability, as measured by precision@k and mean time-to-triage on held-out future alerts.

ideal_debate_resolution.condition: Audit 30 days of production triage logs to measure the distribution of alerts-per-queue processed before analyst refresh. If at least 80% of analyst queues fall within 15–25 alerts worked per cycle, Precision@20 is an adequate primary metric. Otherwise, compare the ranking model's precision at the observed operational alert count and score threshold against the baseline under the same threshold.
ideal_debate_resolution.supports_critique_if: Fewer than 80% of queues fall within 15–25 alerts, and the ranking model's precision at the observed operational cutoff trails the baseline by >3 percentage points.
ideal_debate_resolution.supports_defense_if: At least 80% of queues fall within 15–25 alerts, or the ranking model's precision at the observed cutoff remains within 3 percentage points of the baseline despite differing queue structures.

required_empirical_test.condition: Audit 30 days of production triage logs to measure the distribution of alerts-per-queue processed before analyst refresh. If at least 80% of analyst queues fall within 15–25 alerts worked per cycle, Precision@20 is an adequate primary metric. Otherwise, compare the ranking model's precision at the observed operational alert count and score threshold against the baseline under the same threshold.
required_empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Fewer than 80% of queues fall within 15–25 alerts, and the ranking model's precision at the observed operational cutoff trails the baseline by >3 percentage points.
required_empirical_test.supports_defense_if: At least 80% of queues fall within 15–25 alerts, or the ranking model's precision at the observed cutoff remains within 3 percentage points of the baseline despite differing queue structures.

domain: Enterprise cybersecurity intrusion detection using network flow logs and endpoint/network telemetry from a large corporate environment
hypothesis: For enterprise cybersecurity intrusion detection, a graph neural network built on the entity-relationship graph of users, hosts, IPs, processes, and authentication events will detect malicious activity more accurately than a tabular gradient-boosted baseline trained on the same raw event features, especially when evaluated on future-time windows that contain previously unseen attack campaigns.

ideal_debate_resolution.condition: Analyze historical alert volume thresholds across 3 months of production days. For each day, compute the daily alert volume threshold used by analysts to triage hosts. Identify the dominant operational threshold as the median alert volume selected across days where alert volume falls within ±10% of the median. If at least 80% of production days align with this threshold, then: (1) calculate precision, recall, and recall at FPR ≤ 0.001 for both models at this threshold on the test set, (2) determine whether the relative performance difference between GNN and GBM across these metric deltas exceeds 5 percentage points of recall or 10 percentage points of precision compared to their AUPRC-based ordering.
ideal_debate_resolution.supports_critique_if: The GNN shows higher AUPRC but has ≥5% lower recall or ≥10% lower precision at the dominant operational threshold compared to GBM
ideal_debate_resolution.supports_defense_if: The GNN maintains ≤5% recall and ≤10% precision differences at the threshold compared to AUPRC-based ranking

required_empirical_test.condition: Analyze historical alert volume thresholds across 3 months of production days. For each day, compute the daily alert volume threshold used by analysts to triage hosts. Identify the dominant operational threshold as the median alert volume selected across days where alert volume falls within ±10% of the median. If at least 80% of production days align with this threshold, then: (1) calculate precision, recall, and recall at FPR ≤ 0.001 for both models at this threshold on the test set, (2) determine whether the relative performance difference between GNN and GBM across these metric deltas exceeds 5 percentage points of recall or 10 percentage points of precision compared to their AUPRC-based ordering.
required_empirical_test.supports_critique_if: The GNN shows higher AUPRC but has ≥5% lower recall or ≥10% lower precision at the dominant operational threshold compared to GBM
required_empirical_test.supports_defense_if: The GNN maintains ≤5% recall and ≤10% precision differences at the threshold compared to AUPRC-based ranking

domain: Cybersecurity operations center (SOC) alert triage for enterprise network intrusion detection
hypothesis: For enterprise cybersecurity incident triage, a learning-to-rank model trained on SIEM alerts plus incident-response ticket history will prioritize true incidents more effectively than a binary alert classifier, yielding higher precision in the top-k alerts reviewed by analysts.

ideal_debate_resolution.condition: Measure the historical analyst review budget per hour as the median number of alerts reviewed per queue before handoff/rollover. On the validation set, compute both NDCG@20 and Precision@K for the measured K value. Compare whether the model selected by NDCG@20 would differ from the model selected by Precision@K at that exact K. A 0.05 absolute gap in Precision@K between the top-ranked models determines the threshold.
ideal_debate_resolution.supports_critique_if: If the top-ranked model by NDCG@20 has Precision@K at the measured K less than 0.05 higher, or worse, than a model ranked lower by NDCG@20 but better by Precision@K
ideal_debate_resolution.supports_defense_if: If the model with highest NDCG@20 also has Precision@K at the measured K that is at least 0.05 higher than any other model

required_empirical_test.condition: Measure the historical analyst review budget per hour as the median number of alerts reviewed per queue before handoff/rollover. On the validation set, compute both NDCG@20 and Precision@K for the measured K value. Compare whether the model selected by NDCG@20 would differ from the model selected by Precision@K at that exact K. A 0.05 absolute gap in Precision@K between the top-ranked models determines the threshold.
required_empirical_test.supports_critique_if: If the top-ranked model by NDCG@20 has Precision@K at the measured K less than 0.05 higher, or worse, than a model ranked lower by NDCG@20 but better by Precision@K
required_empirical_test.supports_defense_if: If the model with highest NDCG@20 also has Precision@K at the measured K that is at least 0.05 higher than any other model

hypothesis: Re] Reproducibility Study of “Label-Free Explainability for Unsupervised Models”

planted_issues.description: The claim that the paper’s conclusions generalize beyond the original experimental settings is contestable. Additional experiments indicated that at least one main claim did not extend robustly to datasets outside those originally used, suggesting that the paper’s framing may overstate the breadth of applicability of its methodology.

One experimental claim could not be reproduced on the graph dataset, and the exact cause was not isolated. The report indicates that the original methodology was difficult to extrapolate to other datasets and that one experiment failed to reproduce despite exploratory checks, leaving a dataset-specific robustness issue in the claimed setup.

ideal_debate_resolution.condition: Most core claims were reproduced, but the report states that one claim was not generalizable and another was not reproducible on the graph dataset.

domain: Cybersecurity — enterprise email security using inbound message text, headers, URLs, and sender reputation logs
hypothesis: For enterprise cybersecurity email defense, a transformer model fine-tuned on raw email text plus header metadata and sender-reputation features will detect phishing emails better than a baseline gradient-boosted model trained only on engineered URL, domain, and attachment features, as measured by PR-AUC on a future-time holdout set.

ideal_debate_resolution.condition: Compute precision at the operational recall level (e.g., 90% recall) and false positives per 10,000 messages on the future-time holdout set, comparing the transformer and gradient-boosted models at a fixed decision threshold derived from production policy. If the threshold is not fixed, validate PR-AUC differences only at the same recall operating point.
ideal_debate_resolution.supports_critique_if: Transformer’s precision at the operational recall level is less than 2 percentage points higher than the baseline or results in more than 10 false positives per 10,000 messages
ideal_debate_resolution.supports_defense_if: Transformer’s precision at the operational recall level is ≥2 percentage points higher than the baseline and results in ≤10 false positives per 10,000 messages
ideal_debate_resolution.ambiguous_if: Transformer’s precision is exactly 2 percentage points higher or false positives are exactly 10 per 10,000 messages

required_empirical_test.condition: Compute precision at the operational recall level (e.g., 90% recall) and false positives per 10,000 messages on the future-time holdout set, comparing the transformer and gradient-boosted models at a fixed decision threshold derived from production policy. If the threshold is not fixed, validate PR-AUC differences only at the same recall operating point.
required_empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Transformer’s precision at the operational recall level is less than 2 percentage points higher than the baseline or results in more than 10 false positives per 10,000 messages
required_empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Transformer’s precision at the operational recall level is ≥2 percentage points higher than the baseline and results in ≤10 false positives per 10,000 messages
required_empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Transformer’s precision is exactly 2 percentage points higher or false positives are exactly 10 per 10,000 messages

domain: Retail banking anti-money-laundering monitoring using transaction streams, merchant metadata, and account-network relationships
hypothesis: A graph neural network trained on account-network transaction relationships and merchant/entity links will detect retail banking anti-money-laundering cases with higher precision at the same recall than a tabular gradient-boosted baseline using only per-account transaction aggregates.

ideal_debate_resolution.condition: Compute absolute precision difference between the AUPRC-optimized GNN and the best-performing model at two operational thresholds on the test set: (a) investigator capacity cutoff (top K accounts per day, K=average daily reviewed accounts in prior 12 months) and (b) regulatory recall target (Precision@Recall=0.7). The test compares these precision values for both thresholds and checks if either difference exceeds -0.03 (i.e., the AUPRC model's precision is more than 3 percentage points worse than the operationally optimal model at each threshold).
ideal_debate_resolution.supports_critique_if: The AUPRC-optimized model's precision is worse by more than 3 percentage points at either operational threshold compared to the best-performing model for that threshold
ideal_debate_resolution.supports_defense_if: The AUPRC-optimized model's precision is no more than 3 percentage points worse than the operationally optimal model at both thresholds

required_empirical_test.condition: Compute absolute precision difference between the AUPRC-optimized GNN and the best-performing model at two operational thresholds on the test set: (a) investigator capacity cutoff (top K accounts per day, K=average daily reviewed accounts in prior 12 months) and (b) regulatory recall target (Precision@Recall=0.7). The test compares these precision values for both thresholds and checks if either difference exceeds -0.03 (i.e., the AUPRC model's precision is more than 3 percentage points worse than the operationally optimal model at each threshold).
required_empirical_test.supports_critique_if: The AUPRC-optimized model's precision is worse by more than 3 percentage points at either operational threshold compared to the best-performing model for that threshold
required_empirical_test.supports_defense_if: The AUPRC-optimized model's precision is no more than 3 percentage points worse than the operationally optimal model at both thresholds

hypothesis: Re] Learning Memory Guided Normality for Anomaly Detection

planted_issues.description: Reaching the reported benchmark scores required additional hyperparameter optimization beyond what was sufficiently specified in the paper, indicating that the published methodology did not provide enough detail for straightforward reproduction of the claimed results.

The paper did not clearly isolate which components of the pipeline were responsible for the observed performance improvements, making the central attribution to the memory module and the auxiliary losses difficult to verify from the reported methodology alone.

On the ShanghaiTech benchmark, the memory-based variant exhibited problematic behavior such that the corresponding non-memory variant could perform markedly better, contradicting the expectation that adding the memory module should improve anomaly detection on that dataset.

The released code covered only the prediction-task memory variant, while scripts for reconstruction and non-memory benchmark variants were missing from the original repository at the time of reproduction. This left important parts of the experimental methodology underspecified in executable form and required reconstruction of missing variants before validation with the authors.

ideal_debate_resolution.condition: The report broadly supports the method's reproducibility but documents contestable claims about which components actually drive the reported gains and whether the memory module consistently delivers the claimed benefit.
